example: Your experiences are yours and yours alone.
Some of those experiences have taught you things that are absolutely worth sharing with an audience.
The purpose of a talk is to say something meaningful.
A speaker does not have a proper plan for the talk as a whole.
The throughline of a talk is the main idea that ties together everything the speaker presents.
That doesn't mean a talk must only cover one topic.
The version with the throughline is more exciting for a general audience.
Vulnerability is something to be treasured, not hidden from.
If a talk is a journey, then the throughline is the path that journey takes.
Pick an idea that can be properly explored in the time you have to give your talk.
You can create a short script with every topic you want to cover included in summary form.
The points you do make will have more of an impact.
It will help you filter out anything that doesn't connect to your main idea.
ja: 除外する、フィルターで除く

example: Do I know enough about the topic or do I need to do some research?
A speaking coach named Abigail Tenenbaum recommends testing your throughline out on someone.
Only cover as much as you have time to really explore in depth.
Next up, choose your presentation plan.
ja: プレゼンテーション計画
